items.title: Numbered lists + named tools beat abstract takes
items.whatHappened: p001 ('5 tools we use to ship AI in 30 days') outperformed our generic 'AI is changing everything' baseline by 3x on saves.
items.lesson: German SME engineers save lists of named tools. They scroll past abstract framing.
items.recommendedBehaviorChange: Every educational post leads with a numbered list or a named tool comparison. No abstract 'AI is changing X' openers.

items.title: Founder-voice posts under 100 words underperform
items.whatHappened: p002 (short founder POV, 80 words) got 22% of p003's engagement. Comments asked 'and then what?' - the post stopped at the setup.
items.lesson: Founder POV needs to land the resolution. Setup + insight, never just setup.
items.recommendedBehaviorChange: Founder weekly notes are 180-280 words. Setup, what we tried, what happened, and the actual takeaway. Cut the cliffhangers.
